operators.name: copy
operators.summary: Duplicate a representation without transferring or terminating the source.
operators.notes: Copy creates another presentation of structure. It does not by itself decide persistence.

operators.name: project
operators.summary: Expose a selected view while omitting other structure.
operators.notes: Projection preserves only the selected surface.

operators.name: embed
operators.summary: Place a structure inside a larger containing structure.
operators.notes: Embedding adds containment or inclusion without requiring transformation of the embedded item.

operators.name: reference
operators.summary: Create an indirect pointer or citation to another structure.
operators.notes: Reference links without copying or embedding.

operators.name: reorder
operators.summary: Rearrange structure without adding or removing declared parts.
operators.notes: Reordering may preserve some structures and break others depending on downstream semantics.

operators.name: split
operators.summary: Separate one structure into multiple derived structures.
operators.notes: Split does not itself determine whether descendants inherit identity.

operators.name: merge
operators.summary: Combine multiple structures into one derived structure.
operators.notes: Merge may aggregate, absorb, or destroy distinctions depending on downstream semantics.

operators.name: collapse
operators.summary: Compress structure into a reduced representation.
operators.notes: Collapse is stronger than projection because it may replace internal structure with summary form.

operators.name: expand
operators.summary: Expose or generate additional internal structure from a prior representation.
operators.notes: Expansion may reveal implicit structure or add derived structure.

operators.name: shift
operators.summary: Move structure across time, location, context, or frame.
operators.notes: Shift is neutral about whether identity survives relocation.

operators.name: version
operators.summary: Create a controlled successor state with declared lineage.
operators.notes: Versioning differs from branching because it declares a sequential successor.

operators.name: branch
operators.summary: Create multiple divergent continuities from a source.

operators.name: revert
operators.summary: Return toward a prior declared state.
operators.notes: Revert references an earlier state but does not erase intervening history unless specified elsewhere.

operators.name: bind
operators.summary: Attach a constraint, condition, context, or dependency.

operators.name: unbind
operators.summary: Remove a constraint, condition, context, or dependency.

operators.name: authorize
operators.summary: Declare that a transformation pathway is permitted.

operators.name: attest
operators.summary: Assert that a structure, state, or transformation has evidentiary standing.
operators.notes: Attestation is observational. It does not create the thing attested.
